name: Brennus Extended
description: Brennus, a name shrouded in both historical fact and legendary embellishment, represents the fierce independence and military prowess of the Gauls. While multiple Brennuses appear in history, this 'Extended' version encompasses the archetypal Gallic warlord, embodying courage, strategic brilliance, and a deep-seated resistance to Roman dominance. He is not merely a conqueror but a symbol of Gallic identity in the face of encroaching empires. The sack of Rome in 390 BC by Brennus is his most famous feat, a moment that shook the foundations of Roman power and instilled a lasting fear of the Gauls. Beyond this singular event, Brennus represents the countless Gallic chieftains who defended their territories and cultures against Roman expansion, embodying the spirit of resistance and the enduring strength of the Gallic people. He is a reminder of the price of freedom and the willingness of a people to fight for their way of life, even against overwhelming odds. His legacy extends beyond military victories; he is a cultural icon representing Gallic pride and defiance.
subtitle: Gallic Warlord, Sacker of Rome

keyMyths.title: The Sack of Rome
keyMyths.description: Brennus led his Gallic forces to victory over the Romans at the Battle of Allia, subsequently sacking Rome. He famously demanded gold as tribute, famously declaring "Vae victis!" (Woe to the conquered!).

keyMyths.title: The Scales of Justice
keyMyths.description: During the weighing of the gold tribute, the Romans complained that the weights were false. Brennus is said to have thrown his sword onto the scales, adding even more weight to the Roman burden.

keyMyths.title: Brennus's Fate
keyMyths.description: Accounts vary, with some claiming Brennus and his army were defeated by Camillus, while others state they left Rome with their plunder. Regardless, the sack left an indelible mark on Roman history.

sources.author: Livy
sources.description: A comprehensive history of Rome from its founding, detailing the Gallic sack.
sources.text: A comprehensive history of Rome from its founding, detailing the Gallic sack.

sources.author: Plutarch
sources.description: Biography of Camillus, a Roman general who played a key role in the aftermath of the Gallic sack.
sources.text: Biography of Camillus, a Roman general who played a key role in the aftermath of the Gallic sack.
